title: Dimension Hierarchy
description: Describes the tree structure of a hierarchical dimension, such as administrative boundaries or coded indicator trees. Two strategies are defined: 'leveled' for hierarchies where the tree structure is imposed by named level definitions (the hierarchy is not encoded in the data itself), and 'recursive' for hierarchies where each member carries a parent reference in its own fields.

oneOf.title: Leveled hierarchy
oneOf.description: The hierarchy is defined by named levels. Each level specifies which generator output field serves as the member identifier and which field contains the cross-level parent reference. Generator parameters per level filter members to that level. This strategy generalizes server-side predicate-based hierarchies (e.g., SQL condition-based level partitioning) into a backend-agnostic form: conditions are expressed as generator parameters encoded in each level's values_href.

levels.description: Ordered list of hierarchy levels, from coarsest (index 0) to finest.

id.description: Unique identifier for this level within the hierarchy (e.g., 'L0', 'country', 'adm1').

size.description: Number of distinct members at this level. If omitted, clients retrieve count from the generator's /extent endpoint.

parent_level.description: The id of the parent level in this hierarchy. Absent for the root level. Corresponds to geoid's parent_hierarchy_id.

member_id_property.description: The property in the generator's output object that uniquely identifies members at this level. Corresponds to geoid's item_code_field. Defaults to 'code' if omitted.

parent_id_property.description: The property in the generator's output object that holds the identifier of the parent member (from the parent level). Corresponds to geoid's parent_code_field. Required when parent_level is set.

parameters.description: Generator parameters that, when passed to the /generate endpoint, produce only members belonging to this level. The backend-agnostic generalization of a SQL condition or CQL filter. These parameters are also reflected in values_href as query parameters.

values_href.description: Link to the paginated endpoint for members at this level. Parameters from the 'parameters' field SHOULD be encoded as query parameters on this URI.

oneOf.title: Recursive hierarchy
oneOf.description: The hierarchy is encoded inside the data: each member's generator output includes a parent reference field. The generator handles /children?parent=X and /ancestors?member=X by filtering on this field. Corresponds to geoid's RECURSIVE strategy. Aligns with W3C SKOS skos:broader/skos:narrower semantics expressed as a RESTful paginated interface.

parent_property.description: The property in the generator's output schema that holds the parent member's code. Null for root members. The generator output schema SHOULD declare this field. Corresponds to geoid's parent_code_field and to skos:broader in SKOS vocabularies.

root_filter.description: Optional generator parameter expression that selects root (top-level) members when passed to /generate. Corresponds to geoid's root_condition. If omitted, root members are those whose parent_property is null or absent.

depth.description: Optional maximum depth of the tree. Informational; generators MAY enforce this limit.
